sections.title: What belongs here
sections.summary: MOBAs, hero shooters, competitive action games, and any multiplayer game where fairness, replication, prediction, and authority boundaries define the player experience.

sections.title: Core structural rule
sections.summary: Authority must be explicit. Server truth, client prediction, replicated state, player identity, match state, and ability/effect resolution need clear roles and vocabulary.

sections.title: Preferred project structure
sections.summary: - match and rules authority - replicated game state - player state and identity - ability/effect/attribute systems - client prediction and reconciliation - anti-cheat and trust bou

sections.title: Source-of-truth boundaries
sections.summary: Canonical sources should define: - match rules and modes - hero, ability, and item data - authoritative effect resolution rules - ranked or competitive tuning values - environment

sections.title: Verification model
sections.summary: Verification must go beyond correctness in isolation. Temper should encourage: - replication and authority tests - prediction mismatch tests - ability/effect integrity checks - exp

sections.title: Release and hotfix concerns
sections.summary: This family has unforgiving fairness costs. Bugs are not merely local defects; they can create competitive illegitimacy, abuse, or public loss of trust. Before shipping: - verify s

sections.title: Failure modes
sections.summary: - authority split across too many layers - prediction that masks server truth too aggressively - player state stored in the wrong replicated surface - exploit surfaces created by o

sections.title: Team operating notes
sections.summary: This family needs strict ownership for gameplay authority, backend/infra, and security. Multiple contributors can work in parallel, but risky changes should converge behind review

sections.title: Best Temper overlays
sections.summary: - `Kaplan 🎯` for fairness and player trust - `Carmack 🔧` for authority, latency, and correctness - `Miyamoto ✨` for readability in combat and onboarding
